tasks.title: Scaffold Expo PWA tabs shell and navigation
Implement typed analytics foundation
Build onboarding funnel skeleton with donation CTA route
Fix analytics TypeScript errors and wire provider
Design system tokens and card component library
Home screen MVP with hero carousel
Library screen with audio playback
Location service + Prayer times + Islamic date (Aladhan API)
acceptance_criteria: src/lib/location/use-location.ts hook returns { coords, city, country, timezone, status, refresh }
src/lib/location/location-provider.tsx wraps expo-location with permission request + caching to AsyncStorage
Manual city fallback picker with API search via OpenStreetMap

src/lib/prayer/aladhan-client.ts fetches from https://api.aladhan.com/v1/timings/{timestamp}?latitude=X&longitude=Y&method=2, caches response 24h in AsyncStorage keyed by lat/lng rounded to 2 decimals
src/lib/prayer/use-prayer-times.ts hook returns { times, currentPrayer, nextPrayer, countdown, isLoading }
countdown updates every 60s via setInterval, compares wall clock vs prayer time strings
src/lib/prayer/use-islamic-date.ts hook returns { hijriDay, hijriMonth, hijriYear, formatted }
src/lib/prayer/types.ts exports PrayerName, PrayerTimes, IslamicDate types
useQiblah hook calculates bearing to Kaaba using atan2 formula
npm install expo-location
npm run typecheck passes with 0 errors
tasks.implementation_notes: Aladhan API is free, no key needed. OpenStreetMap Nominatim for geocoding. method=2 is ISNA. Response includes timings.Fajr through timings.Isha, date.hijri. Cache with key `prayer_${lat.toFixed(2)}_${lng.toFixed(2)}_${dateString}`. Qiblah bearing formula: atan2(sin(kaaba_lng - user_lng) * cos(kaaba_lat), cos(user_lat) * sin(kaaba_lat) - sin(user_lat) * cos(kaaba_lat) * cos(kaaba_lng - user_lng))

tasks.title: Ambient audio layer for library (bundled loops + mixer)
Tools screen: Tasbih counter and Qiblah compass
acceptance_criteria: Tools tab shows 2 entry cards (Tasbih, Qiblah) using UtilityCard from @/src/components/cards
Tasbih: full-screen dark tap surface, large count display, haptic on each tap via expo-haptics, reset button with confirmation
Tasbih count persists across app restarts via AsyncStorage key 'tasbih_count'
Qiblah: imports useLocation hook from @/src/lib/location/use-location (from T-011), calculates bearing to Kaaba (21.4225, 39.8262) using atan2 formula
Qiblah: shows rotating arrow using expo-sensors magnetometer for device heading, subtracts device heading from Qiblah bearing
Analytics: TOOLS_TASBIH_STARTED, TOOLS_TASBIH_COMPLETED (with count), TOOLS_TASBIH_RESET, TOOLS_QIBLAH_STARTED
npm install expo-haptics expo-sensors
npm run typecheck passes with 0 errors
tasks.implementation_notes: Qiblah bearing formula: bearing = atan2(sin(kaaba_lng - user_lng) * cos(kaaba_lat), cos(user_lat) * sin(kaaba_lat) - sin(user_lat) * cos(kaaba_lat) * cos(kaaba_lng - user_lng)). Convert to degrees. The useLocation hook from T-011 provides { coords: { latitude, longitude } }. If T-011 is not yet merged, create a temporary mock that returns hardcoded NYC coords and import from the same path so it works once T-011 lands. Use theme tokens from @/src/theme for all colors. Use fontFamily from @/src/theme for all text.

tasks.title: Journey screen: Ramadan 30-day tracker
acceptance_criteria: Journey tab shows 30-day grid (6x5 or similar) with numbered day cells
Tapping a day toggles completion state (checkmark or glow effect)
Streak counter at top: 'X Days of Nur' showing consecutive completed days
Import useIslamicDate from @/src/lib/prayer/use-islamic-date (from T-011) to display current Ramadan day number
All completion data persists in AsyncStorage under key 'ramadan_2026_progress' as JSON array of booleans
ShareCard component from @/src/components/cards used for Day 30 share prompt
Analytics: JOURNEY_DAY_COMPLETED, JOURNEY_STREAK_MILESTONE (at 3, 7, 14, 30 days)
Use theme tokens from @/src/theme for all colors, no hardcoded values
npm run typecheck passes with 0 errors
tasks.implementation_notes: If T-011 is not merged yet, create a temporary mock for useIslamicDate that returns { hijriMonth: 'Ramadan', hijriDay: 1 }. The ShareCard component exists at @/src/components/cards/ShareCard.tsx. Ramadan 2026 starts approximately Feb 18. Use AsyncStorage for persistence, no backend needed.

tasks.title: PWA config, app icon, and splash screen
Stripe Payment Link integration for donations
acceptance_criteria: Replace STRIPE_DONATION_URL placeholder with real Stripe Payment Link
Use ?prefilled_amount={cents} query param to pass selected amount
Migrate hardcoded colors in donate-screen.tsx to theme tokens
tasks.implementation_notes: Blocked on lead creating Stripe Payment Link with 'customer chooses amount' enabled. URL format: https://buy.stripe.com/xxx?prefilled_amount=1500 (cents). No backend needed.

tasks.title: Fix T-013 ambient audio: native asset loading + file size
acceptance_criteria: useAmbientPlayer.ts uses require() or expo-asset Asset.fromModule() instead of relative URI string for audio sources
Audio files are <200KB each (re-encoded to 15s, 64kbps mono)
npm run typecheck passes with 0 errors
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented native requirements and optimized assets.

tasks.title: Fix T-008 Qiblah: use Location.watchHeadingAsync for True North
acceptance_criteria: Replace raw Magnetometer implementation with Location.watchHeadingAsync()
Use `trueHeading` from the event for accurate Kaaba alignment (Magnetometer gives magnetic north)
Handle permissions for location services appropriately (fallback to magnetic heading if location denied? or just error state)
Ensure smooth rotation animation (useReanimated or LayoutAnimation)
tasks.implementation_notes: Raw Magnetometer data is noisy and points to Magnetic North. Location.watchHeadingAsync provides True North which is required for accurate Qiblah direction. The current implementation is functionally correct for magnetic north but geographically inaccurate.

tasks.title: Rework Journey hub and My Streaks interaction flow
acceptance_criteria: Journey hub removes routine/reminder setup UI and focuses on a premium progress hero, strong My Streaks entrypoint, and share surface
Dedicated streak page title becomes 'My Streaks' with calm user-facing copy only
Users can mark Fajr, Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib, and Isha individually on the streak page
Daily Salah completion is derived from all five prayers, and Salah streaks update from that derived state
Quran, Fasting, and Dhikr remain available as daily streaks with improved interaction design
Existing Journey state migrates safely to the new per-prayer Salah data shape
npm run typecheck passes
npm run check:typography passes
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep changes within src/features/journey/** only. Remove routine from active Journey UX for now without designing future Settings. Rework visuals toward a more tactile, rewarding feel inspired by the Tasbih tool surface.

tasks.title: Polish Tools and Journey page titles and streak tags
acceptance_criteria: Journey hub gets a top page title treatment aligned with the Tools page header rhythm
The redundant 'Practice' label is removed from the main Tools hero card
The redundant 'Journey' label is removed from the Journey hero card
Journey streak gateway pills use appropriate emojis for Salah, Quran, Fasting, and Dhikr
npm run check:typography passes
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep the diff UI-only and limited to the existing tools tab screen plus journey feature components. Preserve current layout behavior and only remove redundant secondary labels while tightening the page hierarchy.

tasks.title: Center home hadith quote and strengthen share CTA

The share control is moved below the quote/source block

Only the hadith card surface changes
npm run typecheck passes
npm run check:typography passes
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep the diff contained to src/features/home/HomeHadithCard.tsx. Preserve current share behavior and state labels while making the bottom CTA clearer and easier to notice.

tasks.title: Rebalance Tools and Journey card hierarchy
acceptance_criteria: Tools hero card keeps its inviting visual identity while reducing dead whitespace through tighter layout and compact motivating copy
Journey hero card is redesigned with a warmer, more tactile card composition that feels closer in pull to the Tools hero
Journey My Streaks gateway is redesigned so it no longer reads as a plain stats panel and instead feels premium and inviting
No route, analytics, or state-model changes are introduced
npm run typecheck passes
npm run check:typography passes
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep the redesign contained to the existing Tools tab hero plus Journey hub surfaces. Borrow the warmth and contrast of the Tools card, but do not duplicate it exactly. Preserve current navigation and behavior while improving hierarchy, density, and emotional pull.

tasks.title: Re-baseline repo docs and scaffold imaan backend foundation
acceptance_criteria: Root product docs reflect the imaan.app refactor rather than the legacy Path of Nur PWA roadmap
Collaboration state includes the new product direction, backend/platform decisions, and a fresh task queue for the refactor
A root PRD file exists in-repo for agents and humans to reference
Shared backend contracts are scaffolded for intervention, ledger, follow-up, and profile flows
Native app code has typed backend/auth client seams without wiring the old UI into them yet
A server package exists for Azure Container Apps with Supabase/OpenRouter configuration and initial route scaffolding
npm run typecheck passes
npm --prefix server run typecheck passes
tasks.implementation_notes: This is a repo and architecture reset task. Do not attempt the full UI refactor here. Establish the operating docs, shared contracts, app-side API boundaries, and server scaffold only.

tasks.title: Implement native onboarding account creation flow
Build Home intervention transaction flow
Build Ledger and Journey memory surface
Build Profile utility surface and settings shell
Build corpus ingestion and embedding pipeline
acceptance_criteria: Shared contracts define the lean runtime retrieval passage shape used for request-time citation and vector search
Server package contains local corpus processing scripts for Quran and hadith normalization, embedding generation, and Supabase seeding
Runtime schema is documented as a single retrieval_passages table plus vector index, without review workflow tables
Hadith processing defaults to explicit collection whitelisting because the chosen hadith dataset does not expose per-hadith grade metadata; the default seed scope is Bukhari and Muslim
Embedding generation happens before seed write and is provider-configurable through server-side environment variables
A developer can run the full local flow from source JSON to Supabase upsert using documented commands
npm --prefix server run typecheck passes
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep this intentionally lean. Do not build review/admin workflow tables. Assume manual human review happens outside the runtime system and only final retrieval rows are seeded into Supabase. The currently selected hadith dataset lacks per-hadith grade metadata, so the safe default is an explicit collection whitelist rather than synthetic grade inference.

tasks.title: Reorganize repo into apps/packages monorepo
acceptance_criteria: Expo app moves into apps/mobile with working scripts and intact asset resolution
Fastify server moves into apps/api
Shared runtime contracts move into packages/contracts
A Vite landing page exists in apps/landing
Root becomes workspace orchestration only with workspace-aware scripts
Pending collab tasks and ownership rules are updated to the new paths
npm run typecheck passes from root
npm run check:typography passes from root
npm run landing:build passes
npm run api:typecheck passes
tasks.implementation_notes: This is a structural cleanup task. Rehome the mobile app, api, landing page, and shared contracts without mixing in new product feature work. Preserve current behavior and retarget future tasks to the new layout immediately.

tasks.title: Set up monorepo-safe Vite landing page app
acceptance_criteria: Repo supports a separate landing page app in its own deployable folder without disturbing the existing Expo app layout
A new Vite landing page exists under apps/landing and can be deployed by setting that folder as the Vercel root
Landing page uses the existing brand icon/favicon assets and reflects the imaan.app product direction
Root package scripts expose landing dev/build commands
npm run landing:build passes
tasks.implementation_notes: Superseded by T-20260310-008, which performs the full monorepo cleanup and landing-page setup together.

tasks.title: Build landing waitlist one-pager
acceptance_criteria: Landing page becomes a clean single-page waitlist surface aligned with the Path of Nur visual direction
Hero includes a strong CTA, a phone-stage mock, and explicit coming-soon App Store messaging
Primary action is an email notify form with client-side validation and clear success/error states
Signup flow is spreadsheet-ready via a configurable webhook endpoint without introducing backend coupling into the landing app
landing:build passes
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep the scope inside apps/landing only. Use the existing brand assets and current dark premium visual language. Implement a webhook-based submit adapter so the notify flow can later append rows into a simple spreadsheet automation without changing the page UX.

tasks.title: Wire api env aliases and run first live corpus seed
acceptance_criteria: API config accepts the currently provisioned local env variable names without forcing secret renames
Corpus prepare flow can run end-to-end from the monorepo api package using the configured embedding provider
Supabase retrieval_passages SQL is applied or a ready-to-run documented path is verified against the current project
A first live seed pass is executed or the remaining blocker is narrowed to an exact external permission/credential issue
apps/api typecheck passes
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep this slice focused on making the real seed path work in the current monorepo layout. Prefer env alias support over forcing .env churn. Do not widen into request-time retrieval yet.

tasks.title: Implement stable retrieval API route against seeded corpus
acceptance_criteria: packages/contracts defines typed request and response schemas for passage retrieval
apps/api exposes POST /v1/retrieve and validates payloads with shared contracts
The route embeds the query, calls the match_retrieval_passages RPC, and returns citation-ready matches
Zero-match results return a valid empty response shape with 200 status
apps/api typecheck passes and the route works against the currently seeded Supabase table
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep this slice stable and reusable. Do not widen into full intervention generation yet. Prefer OpenRouter-compatible embeddings as the practical default because the Google free-tier embedding path hit quota limits during live seeding.

tasks.title: Normalize contracts package resolution and implement first intervention endpoint
acceptance_criteria: packages/contracts resolves cleanly from apps/api without a local bridge file in task worktrees
The contracts package exports a stable built/runtime-safe surface for workspace consumers
apps/api removes the temporary local contracts bridge and imports shared contracts directly
apps/api exposes a non-501 POST /v1/interventions route with typed request and response validation
The intervention route performs retrieval against the live Supabase corpus and returns a structured cited payload
Root and workspace typecheck pass for contracts and api
tasks.implementation_notes: Start by fixing package resolution correctly so the backend can keep building without worktree-specific import hacks. Keep the intervention endpoint lean: retrieval-backed, structured, and cited. Do not widen into full auth/session gating yet.

tasks.title: Persist interventions to Supabase and expose ledger read path
acceptance_criteria: Successful POST /v1/interventions writes a durable intervention record into Supabase
Successful POST /v1/interventions also writes a corresponding ledger entry in the same durable flow
The stored intervention record keeps input text, classified type, generated payload, and cited passage ids
GET /v1/ledger returns persisted ledger entries instead of 501
Shared contracts reflect the persisted ledger/intervention response shape used by the api
apps/api and packages/contracts typecheck passes and the live local route works against the current Supabase project
tasks.implementation_notes: Build persistence on top of the merged intervention route. Keep the schema lean but durable. Do not widen into auth enforcement yet, but structure the data model so user linkage can be added cleanly.

tasks.title: Link interventions and ledger records to authenticated user ids
acceptance_criteria: The backend uses a real authenticated actor id for intervention and ledger persistence instead of session-only ownership
Shared contracts and request handling define a stable authenticated context for backend writes and reads
GET /v1/ledger scopes records by authenticated actor id
The mobile auth/session scaffolding exposes the actor identity needed to call the backend consistently
apps/api, packages/contracts, and touched mobile auth/session files typecheck
tasks.implementation_notes: Replace session-only actor scope with authenticated user linkage cleanly. Do not widen into full onboarding UI yet, but align the backend and mobile seams so the identity model is consistent.

tasks.title: Remove legacy session ownership schema and codify no-fallback rule
acceptance_criteria: The Supabase schema no longer keeps actor_session_id on interventions or ledger entries
Fresh and already-migrated environments converge on actor_user_id-only ownership without session fallback behavior
API/server docs no longer frame session ownership as a deprecated or retained path
Repo-wide agent instructions explicitly prohibit unapproved fallback, deprecation, or legacy bridge logic by default
Touched backend and docs surfaces validate cleanly
tasks.implementation_notes: Remove the session ownership column outright rather than preserving it as a nullable legacy path. Also tighten the repo instruction surface so agents stop adding defensive fallback/deprecation scaffolding unless explicitly approved.

tasks.title: Rebuild Home as intervention-first entry surface
acceptance_criteria: Legacy Home dashboard/carousel/timeline surface is replaced by a minimalist intervention-first Home
Home keeps only minimal top context and a single primary multiline composer with calm supporting copy and subtle motion cues
Submit path is wired to the existing intervention backend contract without expanding into reserved server/auth tasks
Idle, focused-input, loading, success bridge, and error states feel like one continuous surface
npm run typecheck passes and npm run check:typography passes
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep changes inside apps/mobile/src/features/home only. Do not mutate the active auth/session or api tasks; if backend session/auth assumptions need adaptation, handle them locally inside the Home feature for this pass.

tasks.title: Implement authenticated persisted intervention resolution
acceptance_criteria: POST /v1/interventions/:id/resolve persists a resolution update for the authenticated actor
Resolution writes are scoped by actor_user_id and cannot mutate another user's intervention
The intervention record and ledger entry stay consistent after resolution updates
The route returns a typed success response on the happy path and a clear not-found or auth-scoped failure when appropriate
apps/api typecheck passes and the live local route works against the current Supabase project
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep this slice inside apps/api and the collab state files only. Build on the authenticated actor model already merged. Do not widen into follow-up workflows or classifier changes.

tasks.title: Replace heuristic intervention typing with dedicated classifier
acceptance_criteria: apps/api no longer derives intervention type from a local keyword heuristic
A dedicated classification step runs before generation and returns one of contextual_anchor, quick_validation, or concise_ruling
Classification failures are surfaced distinctly and fail the intervention request closed
The intervention creation path uses the classifier output consistently for generation and persistence
apps/api typecheck passes
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep this slice tightly scoped to apps/api and collab state. Do not widen into follow-up workflows, UI changes, or corpus schema changes.

tasks.title: Disable onboarding as app launch entrypoint
acceptance_criteria: The native app no longer launches into the onboarding stack by default
Root app entry redirects directly into the main tab experience instead of onboarding
Onboarding screens remain in the repo but are inactive unless explicitly navigated to later
apps/mobile typecheck passes and typography validation remains green
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep this hotfix scoped to the mobile app root entry files only. Do not redesign onboarding or the Home surface in this task.

tasks.title: Simplify mobile tab shell to journey home settings
acceptance_criteria: Top-level tabs render only Journey, Home, and Settings in that order
Home remains the default tab and the floating capsule is tuned for three larger icons with improved whitespace balance
A new Settings tab route exists with lightweight links into Tasbih and Qiblah
Library and Tools leave the tab bar without breaking their deeper routes
apps/mobile typecheck passes and typography validation remains green
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep this pass focused on tab structure, icon scale, and a minimal Settings shell. Do not add fold/composer animations in this task.

tasks.title: Make apps/api Azure deployable from monorepo
acceptance_criteria: packages/contracts and apps/api build to production JS artifacts for container runtime use
apps/api production start runs compiled JS with node instead of tsx
A repo-root-context Docker build exists at apps/api/Dockerfile and produces a runtime image for the API
apps/api includes API-specific deployment docs and env example for Azure Container Apps
The repo includes an Azure CLI deployment path for one API Container App and documents required external prerequisites
apps/api typecheck and build pass
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep this scoped to the API container app deployment path. Do not add CI/CD, workers, or fallback runtime behavior. OpenRouter structured-output support remains a hard requirement for intervention generation.

tasks.title: Add explicit dev bearer-token path for pre-auth UI testing
acceptance_criteria: The API can authenticate one explicit dev bearer token as one fixed configured actor id while keeping normal Supabase bearer auth as the default path
The bypass is disabled by default and still requires an Authorization bearer token rather than allowing anonymous writes
The mobile session seam can intentionally provide the configured dev bearer token for local UI testing when no real session exists
API and mobile env examples document the required variables without committing any real token, UUID, or deployment identifier
apps/api typecheck and apps/mobile typecheck pass
tasks.implementation_notes: Keep this explicit and dev-only. Do not add anonymous access, implicit fallback behavior, or fake persistence. The dev actor id may be any fixed UUID because actor_user_id is not constrained to auth.users in the current schema.
